summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with a reading of Psalm 23, a passage he has cherished since childhood. He acknowledged that life is filled with highs, lows, and plateaus, but declared that God remains faithful and sovereign in every season. The central message, titled 'Victory in the Valley,' proclaimed that God's greatest victories often occur in our lowest places. Pastor Travis taught that valleys are not permanent destinations but temporary passages where we encounter God's presence, provision, preparation, and protection. He explained that in the 'valley of the shadow of death,' the shadow represents fear, but it is not reality; the light of Christ is always present. Using the metaphor of God as our Shepherd and us as sheep, he emphasized our need for divine guidance and defense. The sermon concluded with a powerful declaration of God's anointing, which comes through times of crushing, and a prophetic encouragement that blessings of goodness and mercy follow testing. The call was to trust God in the valley, knowing He is fighting our battles and setting us apart for His purpose.
mainPoints: God's presence is with us in the valley, turning religion into a personal relationship where we encounter Him most deeply.
God provides for and protects us in the valley, acting as our Shepherd who meets every need and defends us from our enemies.
God prepares and anoints us in the valley, using difficult times to mature our character and set us apart for His mission.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.'
The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full. I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me—
My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.
For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
The Lord will fight for you; you need only to be still.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 (NIV) - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.'
Monday.action: Identify one area of fear or anxiety you are facing. Write it down and then write 'God is with me' next to it as a declaration of faith.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that I am not alone in this valley. Your presence comforts and protects me. Help me to walk in that truth today.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 23:1 (NIV) - 'The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.'
Tuesday.action: Take a moment to list three specific needs (emotional, physical, relational, or financial). Silently surrender each one to God, your Shepherd.
Tuesday.prayer: Shepherd God, I declare that you are my provider. I trust you to supply all my needs according to your riches. My hope is in you.

Wednesday.scripture: James 1:2-4 (NIV) - 'Consider it pure joy... whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance.'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on a current difficulty. Instead of asking 'Why?', ask 'What can you teach me, God, through this?'
Wednesday.prayer: Father, help me to see my trials through your eyes. Grow my character and deepen my roots in you. Let this difficulty produce spiritual maturity in me.

Thursday.scripture: Exodus 14:14 (NIV) - 'The Lord will fight for you; you need only to be still.'
Thursday.action: Choose to 'be still' in a practical way. For 5 minutes, sit in silence, refusing to mentally rehearse your worries or plan your defense.
Thursday.prayer: Mighty God, I release the battle I am trying to fight on my own. I choose to be still and trust you to be my defender. Fight for me today.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 23:5 (NIV) - 'You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.'
Friday.action: The anointing comes from crushing. In prayer, offer God a past hurt or current pressure, asking Him to use it to anoint you for His purpose.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I surrender my hurts to you. Take what the enemy meant for harm and use it to set me apart. Anoint me with your power and favor for your glory.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:6 (NIV) - 'Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.'
Saturday.action: Look back over your week. Write down one way you have already seen God's goodness or mercy at work, even in a small way.
Saturday.prayer: Faithful God, I declare that your goodness and mercy are following me. Thank you for the victories, big and small. My hope is secure in your eternal house.
